title: Fixing Per-Card Embed Modals in React — One State, Wrong Video
description: When each card in a list needs to open its own embedded video modal, a single shared boolean state causes every modal to fire at once — always showing the wrong embed. This covers how to fix it with index-based state tracking, so each card opens only its own embed.
slug: Fixing Per-Card Embed Modals in React
author: Amin

blocks.content: ## The Bug & Fix — Documented ### What Was Happening You had **one shared state** for all cards: ```plain const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false); // one boolean for EVERYONE ``` When any card was clicked → `isOpen = true` → **every card** rendered its modal at the same time. With 4 projects, you got 4 stacked overlays. The last one in the DOM won — so you always saw the wrong video. **Think of it like this:** Imagine a classroom with 30 students, and one light switch controls all their desk lamps. You click one lamp → all 30 turn on. *** ### The Fix Track **which** card is open, not just _whether_ something is open: ```plain const [openIndex, setOpenIndex] = useState(null); // null = nothing open // 0 = first card open // 2 = third card open ``` Now when you click card #2: ```plain setOpenIndex(2); // only card 2 knows it's open ``` And in the render: ```plain {openIndex === index && ( // only THIS card's modal shows
